scope: S4096 layer-2 actual-LI sparse-MLA backward dKV path

corrected_read.union_role: compressed-group union is the dKV operand/epilogue axis, not the owner of the whole backward kernel
corrected_read.why_user_correction_is_right: The current 11.1 ms Triton boundary and the desired union path both start from q, kv, indices, lse, and dO. The goal is not to introduce new math or a route-major owner; it is to consume QK/P/grad_logits/scaled-dP while they are hot in the query-major producer, then write dKV by compressed-group union rows.
corrected_read.remaining_issue: The 6.26 ms union result consumed materialized probs and grad_logits. A separate dKV split must either read those slabs from HBM or recompute them. The source-boundary union run recomputes them and therefore lands at 8.22-8.79 ms rather than 6.26 ms.

baseline.current_full_boundary_read: single-token query-major Triton source-boundary sparse-attention backward

materialized_prob_union_slice.shape: S4096 layer2 T8 K512 U256 D64 H64
materialized_prob_union_slice.read: Good dKV slice, but starts after probs and grad_logits/scaled-dP have already been materialized.

source_boundary_query_major_union_t4.shape: S4096 layer2 token_block=4 selected_block=512 union_block=64 H64 D512

decision.reason: The query-major union source-boundary dKV split is correct and narrowly beats the split latency gate, but it still does not produce dQ and dKV from the same hot score/prob page in one owner. It is evidence for query-major union epilogue fusion, not an e2e TorchTitan candidate yet.
next_kernel_contract: Keep the single-token or small-token query-major CTA shape that gives the 1.99 ms no-dKV path.
Build P and scaled-dP once from q, kv, indices, lse, and dO.
Consume the same score/prob page for dQ and compressed-group union dKV before HBM.
Use the forward-known union_route_slots sidecar only as dKV epilogue metadata.
Promote only when the full source-boundary dQ+dKV kernel beats 11.13 ms and then measure TorchTitan e2e TFLOPs.
